title: Construction Breaks Ground At 105 West 108th Street on Manhattan’s Upper West Side
summary: Last week, construction broke ground on 105 West 108th Street, a forthcoming 13-story affordable housing building on Manhattan's Upper West Side. Designed by Dattner Architects and developed by The West Side Federation for Senior and Supportive Housing, the project will yield 84 permanent senior housing units for adults 62 years and older, with 40 units reserved for adults 55 and over who have experienced homelessness and are living with serious mental illness and/or substance use disorder. The upcoming structure is part of phase two of the WSFSSH campus, and is being built on an interior lot by the corner of West 108th Street and Columbus Avenue.
author: Michael Young
